description.task: Implement a Spring Data JPA-style repository with auto-derived query methods. Build a repository where methods like findByName(), findByAgeGreaterThan(), and findByDepartmentAndActive() work based on naming conventions.
instructions: Implement all 5 repository methods using Java Streams with appropriate filter conditions.
Output should list names matching each query.
description.hint: Spring Data JPA derives queries from method names automatically. findByDepartmentAndActive generates: SELECT * FROM staff WHERE department=? AND active=?
description.expectedOutput:
findByDepartment(Engineering):
  Alice
  Bob
  Dave
findByAgeGreaterThan(35):
  Bob
  Eve
findByDepartmentAndActive(Engineering, true):
  Alice
  Dave

description.task: Implement a custom query executor that parses simple JPQL-like query strings and executes them against an in-memory data store.
instructions: Implement all 4 query methods using Java Streams.
findDistinctCategories() should return a sorted list.
Output should match the expected results.
description.hint: In Spring Data JPA, @Query allows writing custom JPQL: @Query("SELECT p FROM Product p WHERE p.price < :maxPrice"). Spring executes it against the database.
description.expectedOutput:
Under $400:
  Tablet($399.99, stock=15)
  Desk($249.99, stock=5)
  Chair($149.99, stock=8)
  Monitor($349.99, stock=12)
Electronics by price:
  Monitor($349.99, stock=12)
  Tablet($399.99, stock=15)
  Phone($599.99, stock=25)
  Laptop($999.99, stock=10)
Avg Electronics price: $587.49
Categories: [Electronics, Furniture]

description.task: Implement a paginated repository that returns results in pages, similar to Spring Data's Pageable and Page interfaces.
description.context: Spring Data's Pageable allows clients to request specific pages: repository.findAll(PageRequest.of(0, 10)). This prevents loading thousands of records into memory.
instructions: In findAll(), sort the articles list using the sortBy field (sort alphabetically if sortBy is 'name').
Use getOffset() and size to extract the correct sublist (handle edge cases with min).
Return a Page with the slice, total count, pageNumber, pageSize.
description.hint: Spring Data's PageRequest.of(page, size) + repository.findAll(pageable) implement server-side pagination. Always paginate API responses returning large datasets.
description.expectedOutput:
Page 1/3: [Article-1, Article-10, Article-11, Article-12, Article-13, Article-14, Article-15, Article-16, Article-17, Article-18]
Total articles: 25
Has next page: true
Page 3/3: [Article-7, Article-8, Article-9]
Has next page: false
